metric: Criterion mean for Rust rows; Python perf_counter p50 for SciPy rows

commands.candidate_hz1: RCH_WORKER=hz1 RCH_REQUESTED_WORKER=hz1 rch exec --json -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/.rch-targets/frankenscipy-cod-b RUSTFLAGS='-C force-frame-pointers=yes' cargo bench -p fsci-fft --bench fft_bench -- fft_helpers/cross_spectral_density --noplot
commands.parent_hz1: rch exec --json -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/.rch-targets/frankenscipy-cod-b RUSTFLAGS='-C force-frame-pointers=yes' cargo bench -p fsci-fft --bench fft_bench -- fft_helpers/cross_spectral_density --noplot

fairness_notes: Same-worker internal A/B used hz1 for parent full-complex route and candidate rfft route.
The parent commit did not have the CSD Criterion row; the same bench harness was applied only in the parent scratch worktree without changing parent library code.
SciPy oracle uses the equivalent scipy.fft.rfft cross-spectrum formula as the fastest matching original-SciPy route, with the full-complex scipy.fft.fft formula also reported.
A discarded current-tree routing run on vmi1227854 measured 53.782 us and 1.6805 ms before parent harness parity existed; it is not used for the decision.
A post-revert confirmation run landed on ovh-a, not hz1, and is recorded only as non-comparable confirmation that the reverted route benchmarks.

post_revert_confirmation.comparability: not used for hz1 A/B; confirms reverted full-complex route runs

revert_summary.reason: Candidate regressed 4096 internally and remained slower than the fastest equivalent SciPy rfft formula on both measured sizes.
